# worktree-rooms

> A git worktree manager with a hospital-room metaphor. Rooms are named worktrees (e.g. `room-1`, `emergency-1`). Each room has a placeholder branch that signals it is free. Assigning real work to a room means checking out a feature/fix branch in it.

Source: https://github.com/anzorb/worktree-rooms
Install: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anzorb/worktree-rooms/main/install.sh | bash`
Config: `~/.config/rooms/config.json`
Help: `rooms --help`

## Core concepts

- **room** — a git worktree directory with a stable name (e.g. `room-1`).
- **placeholder branch** — a branch with the same name as the room; signals the room is free.
- **project** — the basename of the main repo a worktree belongs to (e.g. `myproject`).
- **project/room spec** — the canonical way to address a room when multiple projects exist (e.g. `myproject/room-1`). Bare room names are accepted when unambiguous.
- **free** — room is on its placeholder branch, available for new work.
- **in-progress** — room has a real branch checked out.
- **merged** — the branch's PR was merged into the default branch.

## Commands

### rooms add <path-or-url> <room-name> [branch]

Create a new room backed by a git worktree.

- `path-or-url`: local repo path **or** a GitHub SSH URL (`git@github.com:org/repo.git`). If a URL is given, the repo is cloned to `~/code/<repo-name>` first.
- `room-name`: name for the room and its placeholder branch.
- `branch` (optional): base branch to create the placeholder from. Defaults to the repo's default branch.

```
rooms add ~/dev/myproject room-1
rooms add git@github.com:org/myproject.git emergency-1 main
```

### rooms ls

List all rooms grouped by project. Shows room name, current branch, last commit age, and status.

Status values:
- `[free]` — on placeholder branch
- `🔨in-progress` — active branch checked out
- `🟣 merged` — branch merged into default branch

PR numbers are shown as clickable OSC 8 hyperlinks in supported terminals. CI status emojis: ✅ passing · ❌ failing · 🔄 in progress.

Works offline — falls back to cached data with a "last updated Xh ago" note.

### rooms occupy <project/room> [branch]

Enter a room and optionally check out a branch. **This changes the caller's shell working directory** (implemented via a shell wrapper function). Fails if the room is already occupied.

```
rooms occupy myproject/room-1
rooms occupy myproject/room-1 feature/my-feature
```

Tab completion is available for both `project/room` specs and branch names.

### rooms free <project/room>

Return a room to its placeholder branch, making it available again. Fails if the room has uncommitted changes.

```
rooms free myproject/room-2
```

### rooms move <project/source-room> <project/target-room>

Move the active branch from `source-room` to `target-room`. `target-room` must be free. `source-room` must have no uncommitted changes and must not already be on its placeholder branch.

Internally: frees the source first (to release the git branch lock), then checks out the branch in the target. Rolls back the source if the target checkout fails.

```
rooms move myproject/room-2 myproject/emergency-1
```

### rooms purge [--merged]

Scan occupied rooms and list those whose branch is safe to clean up:
- **merged** — PR was merged into the default branch
- **fully pushed** — all local commits pushed to remote (no unmerged local work)

`--merged` restricts candidates to merged PRs only (skips fully-pushed rooms).

Shows a table and prompts for confirmation (default: No) before making any changes. Frees the room and deletes the local branch on confirmation.

```
rooms purge
rooms purge --merged
```

### rooms config set-base-path <path>

Set the directory where new rooms (worktrees) are created.

```
rooms config set-base-path ~/dev/rooms
```

Updates `rooms_base` in `~/.config/rooms/config.json`. Does not move existing worktrees.

## Configuration file

Location: `~/.config/rooms/config.json`

```json
{
  "rooms_base": "~/rooms",
  "rooms": [
    {
      "name": "room-1",
      "main_repo": "/Users/you/dev/myproject",
      "path": "/Users/you/rooms/room-1",
      "placeholder_branch": "room-1",
      "default_branch": "main"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Shell integration

The `rooms occupy` command requires a shell function wrapper (installed automatically) to change the parent shell's working directory. Completions for `project/room` specs and branch names are registered for zsh (`compdef`), bash (`complete`), and fish.

## Dependencies

- Python 3.10+
- git
- gh CLI (optional — needed for PR and CI status in `rooms ls`)
